hero.title: Designing the future
hero.description: Hi, I'm {name}. A UX Strategist and Frontend Developer creating modular, user-centered digital ecosystems.

work.title: Selected Projects.
work.subtitle: A showcase of strategy, design, and implementation.

paquita-pilates.title: Paquita Pilates — A Studio's Digital Soul
paquita-pilates.description: A pilates studio with a strong soul — and a digital presence that wasn't keeping up. I built the whole ecosystem from scratch: brand identity, UX architecture, and a full-stack booking platform that feels as personal as walking through the door.
paquita-pilates.longDescription: Paquita is more than a studio — it's a mood, a community, a teacher who makes you feel seen. What it lacked was a digital experience worthy of that. I took full ownership: brand discovery, UX architecture, UI design, API, and frontend. The booking system handles real-time class availability, a credit wallet, and role-split dashboards for clients and instructors. Every screen was designed to feel warm and unhurried — nothing like a SaaS product, everything like the studio itself. Built with Vue.js and Ionic, backed by a Node/MongoDB API architected from scratch.

gallery.1: Home screen — class schedule, credit balance, and instant booking in one view
gallery.2: Studio dashboard — desktop view for managing classes, clients, and schedules

1.title: Smart Scheduling
1.desc: Real-time availability with instant confirmation — no back-and-forth, just flow.

2.title: Seamless Anywhere
2.desc: From desktop to smartphone, the experience stays elegant and intuitive.

3.title: Studio Identity
3.desc: Visual language reflecting the authentic warmth and earthy tones of the physical space.

4.title: Dual Perspectives
4.desc: Separate interfaces for clients and instructors, each optimized for their needs.

hiqer.title: HIQER — Persuasion for the Outdoors
hiqer.description: What if your phone made you put your phone away? HIQER turns outdoor exploration into a competitive quest — GPS triggers, XP, leaderboards, and a companion Watch app designed so the screen stays in your pocket.
hiqer.longDescription: The question behind HIQER was genuinely provocative: can a mobile app make you use mobile apps less? The answer required building a persuasive UX layer grounded in Self-Determination Theory and variable reward loops. I mapped three distinct user personas, ran guerrilla field tests, and iterated until the friction disappeared. The Apple Watch companion was the key insight — glanceable cues and haptic feedback meant the phone never needed to leave the pocket. Highest marks. Presented at the HdM Digital Innovation Forum.

gallery.1: Quest map — GPS-triggered outdoor challenges scattered across the city
gallery.2: Active quests — XP tracking, progress bars, and upcoming rewards
gallery.3: Leaderboard — social ranking to spark friendly competition between explorers
gallery.4: Apple Watch companion — glanceable navigation and haptic cues, phone stays in your pocket

1.title: Location Triggers
1.desc: QR spots and GPS unlock contextual content exactly where curiosity strikes.

2.title: Playful Progress
2.desc: XP, levels, and leaderboards transform every walk into an achievement.

3.title: Cross-Device Flow
3.desc: Smartwatch navigation means your phone stays in your pocket while exploring.

4.title: Curiosity Engine
4.desc: Historical storytelling and local secrets revealed at exact coordinates.

kischde.title: Kischde — Trade Within Trust
kischde.description: No marketplace noise. No strangers. Just a circle of people you trust — and the things you no longer need. Kischde is a swipe-first trading app built on the belief that giving should feel better than selling.
kischde.longDescription: Five people. One tight deadline. Real deployment ambitions. Kischde was everything a real product launch feels like — pressure, tradeoffs, and the constant pull between what we wanted and what we had time to build. I led UX system design and owned a large portion of the Vue.js/Ionic frontend. The core challenge: make trading feel nothing like a marketplace. The answer was circles — you only see items from people you've invited, stripping away the anxiety of public listings. Swipe-first browsing kept cognitive load near zero. The backend was built to scale without over-engineering the MVP.

gallery.1: Home feed — items shared within your trusted circle, no marketplace noise
gallery.2: Profile view — your listings, exchange history, and trust connections

1.title: Circle-Based
1.desc: Exchange only within trusted friend groups — familiar faces, zero friction.

2.title: Circular by Design
2.desc: Every trade extends product lifecycles and reduces waste naturally.

3.title: Mobile-First
3.desc: Platform-agnostic architecture. Your items, always within reach.

4.title: Effortless Flow
4.desc: Intuitive gestures for giving and requesting — no forms, no friction.

steal-back.title: Steal Back — Play as Protest
steal-back.description: A 2D pixel-art puzzle platformer where activism meets gameplay. Uncover colonial art theft by day, reclaim history by night. Based on true events that demand to be remembered.
steal-back.longDescription: Steal Back is a game concept that emerged from a research project on colonial art restitution. Rather than write a paper, I designed a playable argument. The day/night cycle is the mechanic: during the day, the player investigates — reading archives, speaking to NPCs, building a case. At night, they act — using stealth and puzzle-solving to recover the stolen objects and return them. Each piece you reclaim triggers a factual summary of the real theft it represents. The pixel art style was chosen deliberately — it codes as 'safe and playful' while carrying genuinely heavy subject matter.

1.title: Day & Night
1.desc: Information gathering transforms into stealth action as darkness falls.

2.title: Puzzle Mastery
2.desc: Environmental challenges demanding strategy, timing, and creativity.

3.title: Historical Grounding
3.desc: Every artwork tells a true colonial story — play to remember.

4.title: Pixel Artistry
4.desc: Hand-crafted retro aesthetics with modern depth and atmosphere.

beast.title: B.E.A.S.T. — The Sound of Power
beast.description: EV charging is silent. Passive. Disconnected from the brand you bought into. B.E.A.S.T. is the answer — a multisensory layer of light, haptics, and sound that turns every charge into a ritual worthy of the AMG badge.
beast.longDescription: Combustion engines had a heartbeat. A roar at ignition. A feeling that said: this is something. Electric vehicles lost that — and AMG owners felt the gap. B.E.A.S.T. (Battery Enhanced AMG Sensory Technology) was my answer: a choreographed feedback layer that plays out across the vehicle's LED strips, a proprietary haptic signature through the charging cable, and an audio landscape that evolves as the battery fills. I designed and ran biometric sensory UX research to calibrate every element. The concept was presented to AMG leadership. Documented in a 60-page research paper.

1.title: Light Choreography
1.desc: Visual charging progress synced with audio — an ambient light show.

2.title: Haptic Signature
2.desc: Vibration patterns matching AMG's pulse, felt through the cable.

3.title: Signature Sound
3.desc: Emotive audio landscapes unique to AMG — the heartbeat reborn.

4.title: Sensed Progress
4.desc: Multisensory feedback transforms waiting into an immersive experience.

emobility.title: E-Mobility Thesis — One Ecosystem
emobility.description: Five products. Five separate apps. Zero shared context. My bachelor thesis reimagined what connected e-mobility looks like when your car, wallbox, and home grid finally speak the same language. Graded 1.0.
emobility.longDescription: Mercedes-Benz customers with a full EV ecosystem — vehicle, wallbox, solar panels, home grid — were managing it all through separate, siloed apps. My thesis asked: what if one interface held all of it? I designed the answer from information architecture down to pixel-perfect mockups: a Dynamic Island integration for live charging state, a home energy dashboard, intelligent departure planning, and a V2L control centre. Twelve competitor products analysed. Expert interviews conducted. Every design decision grounded in research. Developed in cooperation with Mercedes-Benz Customer Solutions GmbH. Graded 1.0.

gallery.1: Home screen — vehicle status, charge level, and energy overview at a glance
gallery.2: Dynamic Island integration — live charging state visible without opening the app
gallery.3: Energy dashboard — home, wallbox, and grid connected in one unified view
gallery.4: Smart charging — departure-time planning optimises cost and green energy usage
gallery.5: Modular menu — each function is its own widget, scaling from glance to full control

1.title: Unified Control
1.desc: One interface for all AMG EQ products — seamless switching, zero confusion.

2.title: Energy Intelligence
2.desc: Smart optimization across devices, from vehicle to wallbox to grid.

3.title: Modular Scales
3.desc: From compact widgets to full dashboards — information density adapts to context.

4.title: Future-Ready
4.desc: Foundation architecture for next-generation connected mobility experiences.

dhbw.description: Development of digitalization concepts for various departments of the Baden-Württemberg Cooperative State University. Project manager for the further development of the campus management system.

mercedes-intern.description: Design of UI/UX concepts for the infotainment system of the vehicles "Mercedes-Benz User Experience" (MBUX) in the entertainment sector — basis for the later B.E.A.S.T. project.

travel.description: Worked at Cosmo Elqui Hostel in La Serena, stayed with a Chilean host family, and traveled through Chile and southern Peru — formative experience for intercultural thinking and design empathy.
